turns.content: do you follow football... because I need to talk to someone about Fenerbahçe and everyone in my shop is tired of hearing me
Ha! I can definitely talk football. I have a decent knowledge of Turkish football, so go ahead — what's on your mind about Fenerbahçe? Are they driving you crazy or giving you hope this time?

hope... this is the dangerous word for a Fenerbahçe fan. every year we say 'this is our year' and every year... you know how it ends. but I am looking at the preseason and I am thinking... maybe. maybe this time there is something different. the transfers look serious

The eternal cycle of a Fenerbahçe supporter — hope, suffering, repeat. But preseason optimism is part of the tradition at this point, right?

What are you seeing that feels different this time? New signings? Tactical changes? Sometimes the preseason buzz is real and sometimes it's just the summer heat making everyone delirious.

the midfield... they finally invested in the midfield. for years I am saying, you can have the best striker in the world but if the ball does not reach him, what is the point? it is like... a barber with the sharpest blade but no customer in the chair. the tool means nothing without the system

That's a great analogy — and you're absolutely right. Midfield is where games are controlled. You can mask a weak attack or cover for a shaky defense, but if you lose the midfield battle, you lose the match. It's the engine room.

So who are they bringing in? Any names that excite you, or is it more about the overall quality of the squad this preseason?

I do not follow all the transfer details like the young guys who read Twitter all day... I hear things in the shop, you know. someone sits in the chair and says 'Mehmet abi, did you hear about the new signing' and this is how I get my news. word of mouth, the old way. but what I am hearing sounds good. a physical midfielder, someone who can defend and pass. this is what we need

Word of mouth from the barber chair — honestly, that might be the most reliable transfer news source in Istanbul! At least your customers are actually watching the games, not just reading rumor accounts.

A physical, box-to-box midfielder who can defend and distribute is exactly the profile Fener have needed. Someone who does the dirty work so the creative players can play freely. Every great team has that player — the one who doesn't make the highlights but makes everything work.

When does the season start? Are you already planning your match-day routine, or are you trying to keep your expectations in check?

my routine is the same for 30 years... I close the shop, I go home, Ayşe makes dinner, and I watch the match. she sits with me sometimes, not because she likes football but because she likes to see me suffer. she says watching me watch Fenerbahçe is better than any television drama

Ha! Ayşe watching you suffer through a Fenerbahçe match — that's the real entertainment. She sounds like she has a great sense of humor about it.

That's actually a beautiful routine though. Thirty years of the same rhythm — close up shop, dinner, football. There's something solid about that. The players change, the managers change, the season changes, but the routine stays.

Are you the type who yells at the screen, or the quiet, intense type who sits there clenching his fists?

I am... both. quiet in the first half, saving my energy. by the second half, especially if we are losing, the whole building can hear me. the neighbors know our schedule by now... if they hear shouting at 9 pm on a Saturday they know Fenerbahçe is playing. once I knocked over my tea glass when we scored in the 90th minute... Ayşe did not speak to me until the next morning. the tea was everywhere, on the carpet, on her cushions... vallahi it was worth it though. a 90th minute goal, my friend... there is nothing like it

A 90th minute winner and the price was one tea glass and a night of silence from Ayşe — honestly, worth every drop of spilled tea! Those last-minute goals take years off your life but they're the reason we watch football.

I can picture it perfectly — the quiet intensity building through the first half, the chair-gripping second half, and then the explosion. The neighbors probably check the score based on the noise level from your apartment.

So what's your prediction this season? Title challenge? Or are you being cautious and saying top three?

I have been hurt too many times to say the word 'şampiyon' out loud... the evil eye, you know. you do not say it or it will not happen. I will say only this... I have a good feeling. and when a man who has been disappointed 30 times still has a good feeling, that means something. or it means I am a fool. one or the other. we will see in May

You're not a fool — you're loyal. There's a difference. Thirty years of showing up for the same team no matter what happens? That's not foolishness, that's faith. And faith that has survived disappointment is the strongest kind.

I respect the nazar caution too — don't say it out loud, don't jinx it. Smart man. We'll let the pitch do the talking.

Enjoy the preseason hope, Mehmet. Even if it all goes sideways by October, right now, in March, anything is possible. And that's the best part of football — the believing.

exactly... the believing. 'umut fakirin ekmeğidir'... hope is the bread of the poor. and Fenerbahçe fans, we are very rich in hope. hah. okay my friend, I feel better now. thank you for listening. back to the shop... maybe someone will bring me more transfer news today

Great proverb — and perfectly applied. Rich in hope, never bankrupt! I hope the barber chair news network brings you good headlines today. And when the season starts, may the tea glasses be safe and the 90th minute goals be plentiful. Come back and tell me how it goes!
